title: Earnings: As of March 31, 2026
content:
The credit union reported net income of $640K as of March 31, 2026. This figure reflects the continued strength of our core lending and investment activities, underscoring the durability of our primary earnings engine. Interest income totaled $1,663K while interest expense amounted to $119K, generating spread income that rose significantly compared to prior periods due to favorable market conditions. These results demonstrate a robust financial position driven by strong asset performance.

Non-interest income totaled $14K and continues to provide a modest but reliable complement to core earnings. The majority of this income is generated from other sources ($14K). While non-interest revenue remains limited, it supports our broader diversification strategy without relying on volatile fee structures or speculative ventures.

Non-interest expense totaled $468K, reflecting continued discipline in managing operating costs aligned with the scale of services we provide to members. The largest component is office occupancy and related expenses ($189K), followed by employee compensation and benefits ($279K). These cost levels remain stable relative to operational needs and are consistent with our investment in member service quality.

Overall Position:
The credit union continues to generate strong earnings supported by interest income as the dominant driver of profitability. Non-interest income plays a supporting role, while expense levels reflect necessary investments in operations rather than structural burdens. Sustaining performance will depend on maintaining disciplined cost management and leveraging our core lending franchise. We remain confident in the institution’s financial trajectory given these fundamentals.
